hypothesis: Incorporating a single active-learning fine-tuning round that adds ≤50 DFT-labeled configurations selected by high predicted stress-tensor uncertainty and reweights the surrogate’s loss to equalize normalized errors in energy, forces, and stresses will preserve the VASP-only MEP space-group symmetry in ≥95% of ML-converged images and reduce subsequent VASP-restart SSNEB iterations by ≥40% for both CsPbI3 β→γ and TiO2-B→anatase transitions while keeping refined barrier errors ≤0.02 eV relative to VASP-only results.
